content.problemStatement:
You have 222 boxes, the first having XXX stones, and the second having YYY stones.

You want the (absolute) difference between the stones of the 222 boxes to be exactly KKK.

Each second, you can take a stone from the 1st1^{st}1st box and put it in the 2nd2^{nd}2nd, or take from the 2nd2^{nd}2nd and put it in the 1st1^{st}1st box.

Find the minimum time till the difference between the stones of the 222 boxes becomes exactly KKK. If it is impossible, print −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first and only line of input contains 333 integers - X,YX, YX,Y and KKK.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum time to get the difference to exactly KKK or −1-1−1 if impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The difference is already 000.
Test Case 2 : It can be shown that it is impossible.
Test case 3 : We can spend 222 seconds, taking one stone from 1st1^{st}1st box to 2nd2^{nd}2nd each time. So, we will have 000 stones in 1st1^{st}1st box and 444 stones in 2nd2^{nd}2nd box after 222 seconds. The difference is exactly 444.
